response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web developer with nearly two decades of experience, but their background is fundamentally misaligned with this Principal Performance Architect role. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU or AI accelerator architecture work, or deep-learning workload optimization. The closest relevant experience is low-latency backend systems in DLang and some C/C++ tooling work, which does not approach the hardware performance architecture depth required at principal level.
strengths: Long, consistent engineering career with a BS in CS and 8+ years of experience, meeting the formal education/experience bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance analysis experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not hardware or performance architecture at principal IC level

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or deep-learning optimization background
